note:
{
  "experiment_overview": "The experiment aims to study the effect of different seed layers (sol-gel and sputtered AZO) on the morphology and performance of vertically oriented ZnO nanorod arrays in inverted small molecule solar cells.",
  "procedures_observed": [
    "A person wearing gloves places a sample into an ultrasonic cleaner, then removes it.",
    "The sample is placed on a glass slide and heated in an oven.",
    "X-ray diffraction (XRD) patterns are taken for samples with sol-gel and sputtered AZO seed layers.",
    "Current density-voltage (J-V) curves are plotted for devices with different seed layers."
  ],
  "materials_and_subjects": [
    "ZnO nanorod arrays, ITO substrates, sol-gel seed layer, sputtered AZO seed layer, glass slides, XRD equipment"
  ],
  "tools_and_setup": [
    "Ultrasonic cleaner, oven, XRD machine, J-V measurement setup"
  ],
  "quantitative_observations": [
    "XRD patterns showing peak intensities at 20-30° and 50-60°",
    "J-V curves indicating higher current density for devices with sputtered AZO seed layer"
  ],
  "key_transitions": [
    "Placement of sample in ultrasonic cleaner",
    "Heating of sample in oven",
    "Taking XRD patterns",
    "Plotting J-V curves"
  ],

    "Current density vs. voltage measurements for different seed layers"
